children.heading: .—Temporary flight restrictions
children.label: 1 .—Temporary flight restrictions
children.text: The Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration shall, upon the request by an eligible entity, temporarily restrict unmanned aircraft operations over eligible large public gatherings.

children.label: A
children.text: the temporary flight restriction would be inconsistent with aviation safety or security, would create a hazard to people or property on the ground, or would unnecessarily interfere with the efficient use of the airspace;

children.label: B
children.text: the entity seeking the temporary flight restriction does not comply with the requirements in subsection (b);

children.label: C
children.text: the eligibility requirements in subsections (c) and (d) have not been met;

children.label: D
children.text: a flight restriction exists to the airspace overlying the same location as the temporary flight restriction sought under this section; or

children.label: E
children.text: the Administrator determines appropriate for any other reason.

children.heading: .—Advance notice
children.label: 1 .—Advance notice
children.text: Eligible entities may only request a temporary flight restriction under subsection (a) not less than 30 calendar days prior to the eligible large public gathering.

children.label: A
children.text: Geographic boundaries of the stadium or other venue hosting the eligible large public gathering, as applicable.

children.label: B
children.text: The dates and anticipated starting and ending times for the large public gathering.

children.label: C
children.text: Points of contact for the requesting eligible entity and the on-scene incident command responsible for securing the large public gathering.

children.label: D
children.text: Any other information the Administrator considers necessary to establish the restriction.

children.label: i
children.text: has previously hosted events qualifying for the application of special security instructions in accordance with section 521 of the Transportation, Treasury, and Independent Agencies Appropriations Act, 2004 (); andPublic Law 108–1991See References in Text note below.1

children.label: ii
children.text: is not enclosed;

children.label: B
children.text: have an estimated attendance of at least 30,000 people; and

children.label: C
children.text: be advertised in the public domain.

children.label: A
children.text: have an estimated attendance of at least 100,000 people;

children.label: B
children.text: be primarily outdoors;

children.label: C
children.text: have a defined and static geographical boundary; and

children.label: D
children.text: be advertised in the public domain.

children.heading: .—Eligible Entities
children.label: d .—Eligible Entities
children.text: An entity eligible to request a temporary flight restriction under subsection (a) shall be a credentialed law enforcement organization of the Federal Government or a State, local, Tribal, or territorial government.

children.heading: .—Timeliness
children.label: e .—Timeliness
children.text: The Administrator shall make every practicable effort to assess eligibility and establish temporary flight restrictions under subsection (a) in a timely fashion.

children.heading: .—Public Information
children.label: f .—Public Information
children.text: Any temporary flight restriction designated under this section shall be published by the Administrator in a publicly accessible manner at least 2 days prior to the start of the eligible large public gathering.
the Administrator authorizes the operation for operational or safety purposes;
the operation is being conducted for safety, security, or compliance oversight purposes and is authorized by the Administrator; or
the aircraft operation is conducted with the approval of the eligible entity.

children.heading: .—Savings Clause
children.label: h .—Savings Clause
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed as prohibiting the Administrator from authorizing the operation of an aircraft, including an unmanned aircraft system, over, under, or within a specified distance from an eligible large public gathering for which a temporary flight restriction has been established under this section or cancelling a temporary flight restriction established under this section.

children.heading: .—Rule of Construction
children.label: i .—Rule of Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to prevent the Administrator from using existing processes or procedures to meet the intent of this section.
